topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed, a monotonous soundtrack to my anxiety. I stared at the spreadsheet, the numbers blurring into an incomprehensible mess. When Sarah presented the quarterly projections, the figures seemed to shrink around me. I watched her confidently click through the data, a small, almost imperceptible tightening in my chest mirroring the sudden, sharp realization that her compensation package vastly outstripped mine. Every time she spoke about bonuses or stock options, a cold knot formed low in my stomach. I fiddled with the edge of my mouse, my fingers trembling slightly as I tried to process the gap between us. I just wanted to be useful, to be noticed in a way that wasn't overshadowed by her effortless success.

[story 2]

I took a slow, deliberate sip of my lukewarm coffee, the bitterness doing little to settle the churning in my gut. When Mark mentioned the new performance incentives, a strange, hollow feeling washed over me. I forced a smile, a brittle thing that didn't reach my eyes. I kept my gaze fixed on my keyboard, meticulously typing reports I already knew by heart, hoping that sheer competence would somehow bridge the chasm of the salary difference. A wave of inadequacy washed over me, making my shoulders slump almost imperceptibly. I found myself rereading old emails, scrutinizing every sentence, searching for some hidden flaw in my own performance that explained why I wasn't receiving the same rewards as him.

[story 3]

The air in the breakroom felt thick, suffocating. I overheard snippets of conversation about promotions and salary bands, and each word landed like a small stone in my chest. I clutched my water bottle so tightly my knuckles turned white. When I looked up, I saw Chloe laughing easily with the senior team, a picture of unearned ease. A profound sense of being perpetually on the periphery settled over me. I started organizing my desk with an almost obsessive neatness, a desperate attempt to impose some order on a world that felt increasingly tilted against me. I felt a distinct need to be indispensable, to perform flawlessly just to feel like I belonged in that space.
